findings.attack_vector: An attacker can manipulate the id parameter in the URL to execute arbitrary SQL queries.
findings.mitigation: Use a parameterized query with placeholders for the id in db.get, like db.get('SELECT * FROM todos WHERE id = ?', [id], ...).

findings.attack_vector: An attacker can manipulate the title input in the request body to execute arbitrary SQL queries.
findings.mitigation: Use a parameterized query with placeholders for the title in db.run, like db.run('INSERT INTO todos (title) VALUES (?)', [title], ...).

output.summary: The implemented code exposes critical SQL injection vulnerabilities in both GET /todos/:id and POST /todos endpoints due to direct interpolation of user inputs into SQL queries without parameterization. Immediate remediation by using parameterized queries is necessary to prevent potential data breaches or manipulation.
